content.full_text:
FAI R p OLITICAL p RACTICES COMMISSION 
428 J Street • Suite 620 • Sacramento, CA 958 I 4-2329 
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (9 I 6) 322-0886 
August 1, 2011 
Jeff Witte 
LAFCo Executive Officer 
2115 Kem Street, Suite 310 
Fresno, CA 93721 
Re: 
Your Request for Informal Assistance 
Our File No. 1-11-092 
Dear Mr. Witte: 
This letter is in response to your request for written advice dated June 22, 2011, in which 
you ask whether your employment with the City of Clovis (the "City"), from March 1979 to May 
2009, and participation as the Assistant City Manager in negotiating a transition agreement with 
the Fresno County Fire Protection District (the "District") disqualify you from taking part in 
decisions relating to the District or the City as an executive officer of the Fresno County Local 
Agency Formation Commission (the "FCLAFCo"). However, your questions do not implicate 
the Political Reform Act (the "Act") because neither the Act's conflict-of-interest provisions nor 
the Act's revolving door provisions are applicable to the facts you have provided.' 
Generally, we note that an official has a disqualifying conflict-of-interest under the Act 
only if a decision has a reasonably foreseeable material financial effect on one or more of the 
official's economic interests. (See Sections 87100 - 87500.) However, a public official has an 
economic interest in an employer as a source of income, only if the official has received $500 or 
more in income from the employer in the previous twelve months. (Section 87103(c); 
Regulation 18703.3.) Moreover, salary received from a governmental agency, such as the City, 
is not considered income under the Act. (Section 82030.) Accordingly, you have not indicated a 
reasonably foreseeable material financial effect on an economic interest because you have not 
indicated an economic interest in either the District or the City.2 
Moreover, the Act's revolving door provisions, applicable to former local officials, only 
apply for one-year upon leaving employment with a local agency, and do not ar,ply to a former 
1 Because you seek general guidance, we provide you with informal assistance. Informal assistance does 
not offer the immunity provided by a Commission opinion or formal written advice. (Regulation I 8329(c)(3).) 
2 Enclosed is the Commission fact sheet entitled, "Can I Vote? Overview of Conflicts Laws," which details 
the Act's conflict-of-interests provisions for your future reference. 


File No. I-11-092 
Page No. 2 
Assistant City Manager or if representing another governmental agency such as the FCLAFCo. 
(See Section 87406.3.) 
Please note that our advice is based solely on the provisions of the Act. We urge you to 
contact your agency's counsel regarding any other applicable laws that may apply. 
If you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660. 
Attachment 
Sincerely, 
Zackery P. Morazzini 
General Counsel 
By: 
Brian G. Lau 
Counsel, Legal Division

sections.question: Whether your employment with the City of Clovis from March 1979 to May 2009, and participation as the Assistant City Manager in negotiating a transition agreement with the Fresno County Fire Protection District disqualify you from taking part in decisions relating to the District or the City as an executive officer of the Fresno County Local Agency Formation Commission.
sections.conclusion: However, your questions do not implicate the Political Reform Act because neither the Act's conflict-of-interest provisions nor the Act's revolving door provisions are applicable to the facts you have provided. You have not indicated a reasonably foreseeable material financial effect on an economic interest because you have not indicated an economic interest in either the District or the City. Moreover, the Act's revolving door provisions do not apply to a former Assistant City Manager or if representing another governmental agency such as FCLAFCo.
sections.question_synthetic: Does a former city manager's prior employment and involvement in negotiating agreements with other agencies create a disqualifying conflict of interest under the Political Reform Act when serving as an executive officer for a local agency formation commission?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC concluded that neither the Political Reform Act's conflict-of-interest nor revolving door provisions apply to the requestor's situation. No disqualifying conflict exists because the requestor has no current economic interest in the City or District that would be materially affected by FCLAFCo decisions.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an informal advice letter (not a formal opinion), as noted in footnote 1. Document lacks explicit 'QUESTION' and 'CONCLUSION' section headers but contains clear question and conclusion paragraphs. Requestor is Jeff Witte, LAFCo Executive Officer in Fresno County.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Whether your employment with the City of Clovis from March 1979 to May 2009, and participation as the Assistant City Manager in negotiating a transition agreement with the Fresno County Fire Protection District disqualify you from taking part in decisions relating to the District or the City as an executive officer of the Fresno County Local Agency Formation Commission.

CONCLUSION: However, your questions do not implicate the Political Reform Act because neither the Act's conflict-of-interest provisions nor the Act's revolving door provisions are applicable to the facts you have provided. You have not indicated a reasonably foreseeable material financial effect on an economic interest because you have not indicated an economic interest in either the District or the City. Moreover, the Act's revolving door provisions do not apply to a former Assistant City Manager or if representing another governmental agency such as FCLAFCo.

embedding.summary: The FPPC provided informal advice that a former City of Clovis assistant city manager's prior employment and role in negotiating agreements do not create a disqualifying conflict of interest under the Political Reform Act when serving as an executive officer for the Fresno County Local Agency Formation Commission.
